# Load the provided JSON files and inspect structure
import json, os, textwrap, re, sys
qfile="/mnt/data/file-HHumqWkCbpLvx5yFcuNaEH-quranic_verses.json"
hfile="/mnt/data/file-GTpsjEG93EG6BAuDqWqgAJ-six_hadith_books.json"

with open(qfile, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    qdata = json.load(f)
with open(hfile, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    hdata = json.load(f)

len(qdata), len(hdata), list(qdata.keys())[:10] if isinstance(qdata, dict) else type(qdata)

# show first few entries
import itertools, json, html
for i in range(3):
    print("=== ENTRY", i)
    print(list(qdata[i].keys()))
    print(qdata[i]['ayah_text'][:200])
    print(json.dumps(qdata[i], ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
    print()

# Implement normalization and search
import re, unicodedata, math
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

def normalize_ar(text):
    # Remove tashkeel (diacritics)
    # Arabic diacritics unicode ranges:  0610-061A, 064B-065F, 0670, 06D6-06DC, 06DF-06E8, 06EA-06ED
    # Simpler: remove characters in regex [\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u0670\u06D6-\u06ED]
    text = re.sub(r'[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u0670\u06D6-\u06ED]', '', text)
    # Remove tatweel
    text = text.replace('\u0640','')
    # Normalize different hamzas to bare hamza like أ إ آ ء etc -> ا? but the instructions said unify hamza; let's map to same char 'ء' maybe.
    # Better map all hamza variants to 'ء' or to 'ا'? We'll map to 'ء' for uniformity.
    hamza_variants = {'أ':'ء','إ':'ء','آ':'ء','ؤ':'ء','ئ':'ء','ء':'ء','ٱ':'ا'} # keep Alef with wasla (ٱ) to ا
    for k,v in hamza_variants.items():
        text = text.replace(k,v)
    # Remove punctuation
    text = re.sub(r'[^\w\sء\u0621-\u063A\u0641-\u064A]', ' ', text)  # keep Arabic letters and wrd/space, hamza; replace others with space
    # Replace Arabic numerals? Keep numbers separate.
    # collapse spaces and to lowercase (Arabic has no case)
    text = re.sub(r'\s+',' ', text).strip()
    return text

def tokenize(text):
    return text.split()

def token_f1(a,b):
    # a and b are normalized strings; compute token-level F1 of overlap relative to a (pred) and b (gold)
    ta = tokenize(a)
    tb = tokenize(b)
    ca = Counter(ta)
    cb = Counter(tb)
    common = sum((ca & cb).values())
    if common==0:
        return 0.0
    prec = common / len(ta)
    rec = common / len(tb)
    if (prec+rec)==0:
        return 0.0
    f1 = 2*prec*rec/(prec+rec)
    return f1

def lcs_length(a,b):
    # longest common substring length (characters) using dynamic programming
    # return length / max(len(b),1)
    m = len(a); n = len(b)
    if m==0 or n==0: return 0
    dp = [[0]*(n+1) for _ in range(m+1)]
    best=0
    for i in range(m-1,-1,-1):
        for j in range(n-1,-1,-1):
            if a[i]==b[j]:
                dp[i][j]=dp[i+1][j+1]+1
                if dp[i][j]>best: best=dp[i][j]
    return best

# Prepare normalized version of all ayah_text for quick search
norm_q = []
for entry in qdata:
    t = entry['ayah_text']
    nt = normalize_ar(t)
    norm_q.append(nt)

full_span = "وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا الْإِنسَانَ وَنَعْلَمُ مَا تُوَسِّسُ فِي نَفْسِهِ وَنَحْنُ أَقْرَبُ إِلَيْهِ مِنْكُمْ وَلَكِنْ لا تُبْصِرُونَ"
snippet = "وَلَقَدْخ..." # not useful, but use snippet provided: 'وَلَقَدْ خ...ُبْصِرُونَ' in prompt they truncated with ellipsis; but "Provided_Span_Snippet" shows "وَلَقَدْ خ...ُبْصِرُونَ"
# We'll use full_span and also another variant: replace 'تُوَسِّسُ فِي' with 'تُوَسْوِسُ بِهِ نَفْسُهُ' to match canonical
norm_full = normalize_ar(full_span)
norm_full, full_span[:80]

# compute top matches by token_f1 and lcs ratio
scores = []
for i,entry in enumerate(qdata):
    cand = norm_q[i]
    f1 = token_f1(cand, norm_full)
    lcs = lcs_length(cand, norm_full)/max(len(norm_full),1)
    # also compute longest common subsequence maybe simpler; but ok
    scores.append((f1, lcs, i, entry['surah_name'], entry['surah_id'], entry['ayah_id'], entry['ayah_text']))

scores_sorted = sorted(scores, key=lambda x: (x[0], x[1]), reverse=True)
for s in scores_sorted[:12]:
    print("F1:{:.3f} LCS:{:.3f} idx:{} surah:{} {}:{} ::: {}".format(s[0], s[1], s[2], s[3], s[4], s[5], s[6]))

# show entries around relevant indices
for idx in [4645, 5063]:
    entry = qdata[idx]
    print("Index", idx, "surah", entry['surah_name'], "ayah_id", entry['ayah_id'])
    print(entry['ayah_text'])
    print()

cand_qaf = qdata[4645]['ayah_text']
cand_waqi = qdata[5063]['ayah_text']
combined = cand_qaf + " " + cand_waqi
print("Qaf:", cand_qaf)
print("Waqi:", cand_waqi)
print("Combined:", combined) 
norm_qaf = normalize_ar(cand_qaf)
norm_waqi = normalize_ar(cand_waqi)
norm_combined = normalize_ar(combined)
print("norm_full:", norm_full)
print("norm_qaf:", norm_qaf)
print("norm_waqi:", norm_waqi)
print("norm_combined:", norm_combined)
print("F1 qaf-full:", token_f1(norm_qaf, norm_full))
print("F1 waqi-full:", token_f1(norm_waqi, norm_full))
print("F1 combined-full:", token_f1(norm_combined, norm_full))
print("LCS combined-full:", lcs_length(norm_combined, norm_full)/max(len(norm_full),1))
